name: ExtensionContentSignatureRDA
title: Extension: Información sobre la firma del contenido
publisher: Ministerio de Salud y Protección Social de Colombia

contact.name: Ministerio de Salud y Protección Social de Colombia
Ministerio de Salud y Protección Social de Colombia

description: Extensión utilizada en el recurso DocumentReference, para registrar información sobre la firma del contenido (documento PDF).
copyright: Ministerio de Salud y Protección Social, CC BY-NC-SA 4.0, 2025+

element.short: Extension
element.definition: Extensión utilizada en el recurso DocumentReference, para registrar información sobre la firma del contenido (documento PDF).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Unique id for inter-element referencing
element.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.short: Información sobre la firma del contenido del documento
element.definition: Extensión utilizada en el recurso DocumentReference, para registrar información sobre la firma del contenido (documento PDF).
element.comment: El proceso de registro de información de firma digital aplicada al contenido (documento PDF) y la validación de la misma, se realiza en la plataforma de interoperabilidad del Ministerio de Salud y Protección Social. Esta extensión permite registrar en el recurso DocumentReference la información relevante sobre la firma digital aplicada al contenido (documento PDF), así como los resultados de las validaciones realizadas.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Registro de la firma del contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Tipo de dato FHIR Signature que contiene la información de la firma digital aplicada al contenido (documento PDF).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Unique id for inter-element referencing
element.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.short: Extension
element.definition: An Extension

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: identifies the meaning of the extension
element.definition: Source of the definition for the extension code - a logical name or a URL.
element.comment: The definition may point directly to a computable or human-readable definition of the extensibility codes, or it may be a logical URI as declared in some other specification. The definition SHALL be a URI for the Structure Definition defining the extension.

element.short: Value of extension
element.definition: Value of extension - must be one of a constrained set of the data types (see [Extensibility](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/extensibility.html) for a list).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Unique id for inter-element referencing
element.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.short: Additional content defined by implementations
element.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.
element.comment: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions.  The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Tipo de firma digital aplicada al contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Indica el tipo de firma digital aplicada al contenido (documento PDF), de acuerdo con las especificaciones ISO/ASTM E1762-95:2013.
element.comment: Examples include attesting to: authorship, correct transcription, and witness of specific event. Also known as a "Commitment Type Indication".

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

binding.description: An indication of the reason that an entity signed the object.

element.short: Unique id for inter-element referencing
element.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.short: Additional content defined by implementations
element.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.
element.comment: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions.  The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.short: Identity of the terminology system
element.definition: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.
element.comment: The URI may be an OID (urn:oid:...) or a UUID (urn:uuid:...).  OIDs and UUIDs SHALL be references to the HL7 OID registry. Otherwise, the URI should come from HL7's list of FHIR defined special URIs or it should reference to some definition that establishes the system clearly and unambiguously.
element.requirements: Need to be unambiguous about the source of the definition of the symbol.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Version of the system - if relevant
element.definition: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.
element.comment: Where the terminology does not clearly define what string should be used to identify code system versions, the recommendation is to use the date (expressed in FHIR date format) on which that version was officially published as the version date.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Symbol in syntax defined by the system
element.definition: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).
element.requirements: Need to refer to a particular code in the system.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Representation defined by the system
element.definition: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.
element.requirements: Need to be able to carry a human-readable meaning of the code for readers that do not know  the system.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: C*E.2 - but note this is not well followed

element.short: If this coding was chosen directly by the user
element.definition: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).
element.comment: Amongst a set of alternatives, a directly chosen code is the most appropriate starting point for new translations. There is some ambiguity about what exactly 'directly chosen' implies, and trading partner agreement may be needed to clarify the use of this element and its consequences more completely.
element.requirements: This has been identified as a clinical safety criterium - that this exact system/code pair was chosen explicitly, rather than inferred by the system based on some rules or language processing.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: fhir:Coding.userSelected fhir:mapsTo dt:CDCoding.codingRationale. fhir:Coding.userSelected fhir:hasMap fhir:Coding.userSelected.map. fhir:Coding.userSelected.map a fhir:Map;   fhir:target dt:CDCoding.codingRationale. fhir:Coding.userSelected\#true a [     fhir:source "true";     fhir:target dt:CDCoding.codingRationale\#O   ]

element.short: Fecha y hora de la firma digital aplicada al contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Fecha (timestamp) en que se aplicó la firma digital al contenido (documento PDF).
element.comment: This should agree with the information in the signature.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Entidad que aplicó la firma digital al contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Referencia a la Institución Prestadora de Servicios de Salud (Organization) o Profesional Independiente (PractitionerRole) que aplicó la firma digital al contenido (documento PDF).
element.comment: This should agree with the information in the signature.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: The party represented
element.definition: A reference to an application-usable description of the identity that is represented by the signature.
element.comment: The party that can't sign. For example a child.
element.requirements: used when the signature is on behalf of a non-signer.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Formato del contenido firmado (PDF)
element.definition: Indica el formato del contenido firmado. En este caso, siempre será application/pdf.
element.comment: "xml", "json" and "ttl" are allowed, which describe the simple encodings described in the specification (and imply appropriate bundle support). Otherwise, mime types are legal here.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

binding.description: The mime type of an attachment. Any valid mime type is allowed.

element.short: The technical format of the signature
element.definition: A mime type that indicates the technical format of the signature. Important mime types are application/signature+xml for X ML DigSig, application/jose for JWS, and image/* for a graphical image of a signature, etc.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

binding.description: The mime type of an attachment. Any valid mime type is allowed.

element.short: The actual signature content (XML DigSig. JWS, picture, etc.)
element.definition: The base64 encoding of the Signature content. When signature is not recorded electronically this element would be empty.
element.comment: Where the signature type is an XML DigSig, the signed content is a FHIR Resource(s), the signature is of the XML form of the Resource(s) using  XML-Signature (XMLDIG) "Detached Signature" form.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Resultado de la validación global de la firma
element.definition: Resultado Booleano que indica si, en conjunto, la validación global del registro/evidencia resultó exitosa. Es decir: si todos los chequeos relevantes pasaron.

element.short: Value of extension
element.definition: Value of extension - must be one of a constrained set of the data types (see [Extensibility](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/extensibility.html) for a list).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: identifies the meaning of the extension
element.definition: Source of the definition for the extension code - a logical name or a URL.
element.comment: The definition may point directly to a computable or human-readable definition of the extensibility codes, or it may be a logical URI as declared in some other specification. The definition SHALL be a URI for the Structure Definition defining the extension.

element.short: Value of extension
element.definition: Value of extension - must be one of a constrained set of the data types (see [Extensibility](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/extensibility.html) for a list).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: Extensión utilizada en el recurso DocumentReference, para registrar información sobre la firma del contenido (documento PDF).

element.short: Información sobre la firma del contenido del documento
element.definition: Extensión utilizada en el recurso DocumentReference, para registrar información sobre la firma del contenido (documento PDF).
element.comment: El proceso de registro de información de firma digital aplicada al contenido (documento PDF) y la validación de la misma, se realiza en la plataforma de interoperabilidad del Ministerio de Salud y Protección Social. Esta extensión permite registrar en el recurso DocumentReference la información relevante sobre la firma digital aplicada al contenido (documento PDF), así como los resultados de las validaciones realizadas.

element.short: Registro de la firma del contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Tipo de dato FHIR Signature que contiene la información de la firma digital aplicada al contenido (documento PDF).

element.short: Tipo de firma digital aplicada al contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Indica el tipo de firma digital aplicada al contenido (documento PDF), de acuerdo con las especificaciones ISO/ASTM E1762-95:2013.

element.short: Fecha y hora de la firma digital aplicada al contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Fecha (timestamp) en que se aplicó la firma digital al contenido (documento PDF).

element.short: Entidad que aplicó la firma digital al contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Referencia a la Institución Prestadora de Servicios de Salud (Organization) o Profesional Independiente (PractitionerRole) que aplicó la firma digital al contenido (documento PDF).

element.short: Formato del contenido firmado (PDF)
element.definition: Indica el formato del contenido firmado. En este caso, siempre será application/pdf.

element.short: Resultado de la validación global de la firma
element.definition: Resultado Booleano que indica si, en conjunto, la validación global del registro/evidencia resultó exitosa. Es decir: si todos los chequeos relevantes pasaron.

element.short: Identificador de la validación de la firma del contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Una cadena (UUID) que representa el identificador único del registro digital creado o referenciado. Permite referenciar de forma inequívoca ese “ítem de evidencia digital.

element.short: Fecha y hora de la validación de la firma del contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Fecha (timestamp) en que se registró la evidencia o en que se realizó el proceso de registro/validación de la firma del contenido en el sistema

element.short: Resultado de la validación de la firma del contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Resulado Booleano que indica si la validación básica de la evidencia (firmas, formato, esquema, etc.) respecto al contenido fue exitosa.

element.short: Resultado de la prueba de validación de integridad de la firma del contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Resultado Booleano de la comprobación de integridad (normalmente hashing, firmas, que el contenido no haya sido alterado)

element.short: Resultado de la prueba de validación de expiración de la firma del contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Resultado Booleano de la comprobación de expiración del certificado usado para firmar el contenido Indica si la validación de fechas (por ejemplo, que el certificado no esté expirado en el momento de la verificación) fue exitosa.

element.short: Resultado de la prueba de validación de confianza de la firma del contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Resultado Booleano de la validación de confianza. Es decir, que el certificado usado para firmar esté dentro de una cadena de confianza reconocida (CA confiable, raíz válida, etc.).

element.short: Resultado de la prueba de validación de listas de revocación (CRL u OCSP) de la firma del contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Resultado Booleano de la verificación contra listas de revocación (CRL u OCSP) para asegurar que el certificado no haya sido revocado

element.short: Identificador de la propiedad adicional en la firma del contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Identificador unívoco de la propiedad adicional registrada en la firma del contenido (PDF).

element.short: Valor de la propiedad adicional en la firma del contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Cadena de texto que contiene el valor propiedad adicional registrada en la firma del contenido (PDF).

element.short: Error detectado en la firma del contenido (PDF)
element.definition: Cadena de texto que contiene el error detectado durante los pasos de validación o registro falla, este campo contendrá un mensaje de error explicando cuál fue el problema (por ejemplo, “certificate expired”, “revoked certificate”, “integrity check failed”, etc.).
